title: Opinion on various Eva mangas
wikitext:
[]: Opinion on various Eva mangas?             Quote Post by bonehead156 » Sat Jun 25, 2011 3:48 pm So what is y'alls opinion on the various Evangelion mangas like Sadamoto's manga or the Ikari Raising Project?

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sat Jun 25, 2011 5:08 pm Record of Heaven's decent: Great! Such a shame it was rushed... Re-take: Best EVA manga. Normal version is a liiiiiiittle bit over-the-top. But the All-Ages version fixes that. Angelic Days: Eww. Shinji Ikari Rasing Project: Quite amusing.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jun 25, 2011 5:10 pm Most relevant threads : [MANGA][NGE] What's the general consensus here? -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6206 [MANGA] Shinji Ikari Raising Project -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8298 Related http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=109 and most of the other threads here http://forum.evageeks.org/thread_lists/Everything_Else_Evangelion__threads.html with "manga" in the title.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Jun 25, 2011 10:19 pm Re-take: The closure for me after the trauma of EoE. Great for LAS (Asuka/Shinji shipping). Arguably consistent with Eva world-view and EoE events. All-Age version is the final edit, so if you want to check it out I recommend All-Age version. For a doujin, still in print after all these years -- testament to its big success Shinji Ikari Rasing Project: Continuation of the fun school comedy from Ep.26' in TV If you love slice-of-life kind of story (plot progress is a bit slow) and want to see heartwarming and humorous side of everybody (incl. Gendou, Yui, Kyouko etc) it is the best place to look. Angelic Days: A series of doujin anthology by many doujin artists. Many hentai, many violent, some funny stuff. Don't think in print anymore Lost Paradise: Ditto N-goki seres: (N being 0,1,2,3...) Ditto Sadamoto's manga is another canon for Eva. I love it but many think various character developments were not done well.... Also the story could be a bit different here and there. Better to read it as an alternative story of Eva and not think about the anime too much.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jun 26, 2011 2:10 am symbv wrote: Sadamoto's manga is another canon for Eva. I love it but many think various character developments were not done well...

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Sun Jun 26, 2011 5:46 am symbv wrote: [..] Shinji Ikari Rasing Project: Continuation of the fun school comedy from Ep.26' in TV [..]

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Sun Jun 26, 2011 10:36 am Sadamoto's manga is BEAST!!! Can't wait to collect all of the volumes!

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sun Jun 26, 2011 10:59 am Campus Apocalypse -- might not be the best, but rather a very interesting reinvention of the universe, and utilizing existing story elements in a new form. Angelic Days -- looked nice/odd at first, but then went downhill.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jun 26, 2011 11:49 am symbv wrote: Angelic Days: A series of doujin anthology by many doujin artists. Many hentai, many violent, some funny stuff. Don't think in print anymore

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Jun 26, 2011 1:56 pm Sadamoto's manga is probably my second favourite comic series. Read it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jun 26, 2011 4:31 pm symbv wrote: Sorry my mistake above -- the doujin anthology is called "Angelic Impact". Grrr.....

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jun 26, 2011 6:53 pm ayy

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jun 26, 2011 8:21 pm Sadamoto: I'm mildly interested in the story changes, but dislike the character development intensely (apart from Rei, at least). Angelic Days: I enjoyed it more than most. There's not much to it, but it holds together pretty well (though the last couple of volumes were kinda screwy given that it ended after V. 4 and then had a bunch of other stuff after that). ISRP: Better overall quality than AD, but less coherent and a lot more fanservice. I thought it got awfully repetitive after the first couple of volumes. Re-Take: The plot twists are laughable and the character assassination of Rei is nigh-unforgivable, but I give it points for subverting the Peggy Sue cliche and showing why the ending of NGE was not, in fact, an error. Also, the AU setting is very cute, particularly at the end. Haven't read the others.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jun 26, 2011 10:43 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: In fact I was wondering to which manga you were referring to. Unfortunately this is out of reach for most of us.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jun 26, 2011 10:54 pm symbv wrote: I goggled "angelic impact doujin" and found it available in a bunch of sites (all 9 volumes of them). You might want to check them out.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jun 26, 2011 11:05 pm ^ that I am not sure as I read the Japanese originals.... I also found some sites having "Paradise Lost" when I searched "paradise lost doujin"...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Jun 26, 2011 11:12 pm symbv wrote: ^ that I am not sure as I read the Japanese originals....

[]:             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Mon Jun 27, 2011 10:17 am Question: What are the mangas that actually features the Evas and Angels? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Jun 27, 2011 11:14 am ^ I always loved that OMAKE!! Shogoki & Zuriel for the WIN!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 27, 2011 12:05 pm I think that omake made me experience moeru . [s]I must know the source.[/s] Nevermind, I figured it out.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sat Jun 25, 2011 5:08 pm Record of Heaven's decent: Great! Such a shame it was rushed... Re-take: Best EVA manga. Normal ver
